title: Genome Editing Tool Profile
description: Canonical record for a single computational genome editing tool. One object per tool. Mirrors the Markdown page one to one. Volatile fields (citations, last_known_update, maintenance_status, state_of_the_art) carry a verified_on date so staleness is auditable.

id.description: URL-safe stable identifier, lowercase kebab-case. Never reused. Example: crispor

name.description: Display name as the authors write it.

aka.description: Alternate names, former names, or common misspellings, for search.

category.description: Primary Phase 1 taxonomy category (single value). Example: Guide RNA Design

secondary_categories.description: Other categories this tool legitimately belongs to. Drives cross-linking.

description.description: Two to four sentence factual summary of what the tool does. No marketing language.

installation.description: How to install or access. Include package manager command if applicable (pip, conda, CRAN, Bioconductor), or 'Web only', or 'No install (hosted)'.

license.description: SPDX identifier where possible (MIT, GPL-3.0, BSD-3-Clause, Apache-2.0), or 'Proprietary', or 'Academic/non-commercial', or 'Unknown'.

maintenance_status.description: Active = updated within ~18 months or clearly supported. Legacy = stable and still used but not actively developed. Superseded = a direct successor exists. Experimental = research preview. Abandoned = no signal of maintenance and known issues.

superseded_by.description: id of the successor tool, if maintenance_status is Superseded.

citations.description: Citation count is volatile. Always store the number WITH its source and date.

source.description: e.g. Google Scholar, Crossref, Semantic Scholar

documentation.description: URL to docs, wiki, or tutorial. Null if none beyond the paper.

limitations.description: Hard boundaries: organisms, editor types, scale, or access constraints it cannot handle.

competing_tools.description: ids of tools that solve the same problem and would be chosen instead of this one.

related_tools.description: ids of tools commonly used alongside this one in a workflow (complementary, not competing).

example_workflow.description: Short numbered or prose walkthrough of a canonical use, input to output.

screenshots.description: Publicly available screenshots or figures. Store URL and source attribution only; do not rehost without checking license.

notable_publications_using.description: High-profile papers that USED the tool (distinct from the tool's own paper).

last_known_update.description: Most recent evidence of development: release, commit, or web tool refresh. Pair with verified_on.

state_of_the_art.description: Whether the tool is still a leading choice for its task, as of the verification date. This is a judgment, so record the reasoning.

tags.description: Free-form facets for filtering: organism scope, interface type, algorithm family, etc. See tag vocabulary.

ai_enabled.description: True if the tool's core method relies on a trained machine learning or deep learning model (e.g. a learned scoring model), as opposed to a purely rule-based, statistical, or brute-force algorithm. Drives the AI-enabled vs traditional facet.

verified_on.description: Date a human or agent last checked the volatile fields against primary sources.

verification_notes.description: What was and was not verified, and where numbers still need a manual pull.
